visual_attributes.morphology: elongated body with a wide head and external gills
distinctive_features: caudal fin extending from behind the head to the vent
underdeveloped limbs with long, thin digits
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: 15 to 45 cm in length, commonly around 23 cm
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: three pairs of external gill stalks behind the head

domain_specific_functional.regeneration: capable of regenerating limbs, gills, and parts of the brain
domain_specific_functional.feeding: suction feeding with interlocking gill rakers

relationships.description: Closest relative within the Ambystomatidae family
Originally inhabited wetlands and lakes in the highlands of Mexico

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Lakes of Xochimilco and Chalco, presumed Texcoco and Zumpango
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Part of the diet of native Mexica; associated with Aztec mythology
